CodificationSection was enacted as part of the Biomass Energy and Alcohol Fuels Act of 1980 which is title II of the Energy Security Act, and not as part of the Natural Gas Policy Act of 1978 which comprises this chapter.

children.text: include use of natural gas in sugar refining for production of alcohol;

include use of natural gas for agricultural production on set-aside acreage or acreage diverted from the production of a commodity (as provided under the Agricultural Act of 1949 [ et seq.]) to be devoted to the production of any commodity for conversion into alcohol or hydrocarbons for use as motor fuel or other fuels; and7 U.S.C. 1421

for the 5-year period beginning on , include use of natural gas in the distillation of fuel-grade alcohol from food grains or other biomass by facilities in existence on , which do not have the installed capability to burn coal lawfully.June 30, 1980June 30, 1980
